Okay, so now that you've identified that you do have a performance issue and it's not on your end or you have done everything possible on your end to make it better, okay? You need to have written documentation like I said from the get-go. So incidents that this would apply to, right? Incidences that you would write somebody up. It might be violation of a handbook, violation of values, or no improvement to issues, right? So I want to provide you all with an example of what be used. And so I'll just kind of walk you through the questions. It asks and this is 100% so that is very clear to every party, meaning to myself, to the teammate that I'm talking to, and to anyone that were to come in and look from a legal standpoint. Exactly what happened, okay? Counseling type. I've made the following observations of the employee's conduct or performance. Writing down, you know, what is it that's going wrong? Prior discussion or warnings on this subject. What have I done to try and make it right? How have I coached this person, right? And then I have informed this employee of the following standards that are expected from him or her. So what have I expressed needs to be done differently. And lastly, these standards are important because of the following impact on the work environment and explaining why this is so vital to their job, right? After that, there is a spot for employee comments. That is where you would then give this to the employee to fill out their comments to this, okay? And then after that, you both sign and you have it documented. You can just store it in a Google folder, okay? And so typically, how we do it is three write-ups means that it's a termination. However, seldom does it get to that point. I think we've only had, you know, one to two people who have actually gone through all three. But this is incredibly important because if somebody is going to claim that you are doing something wrong, you have done them wrong. If you have documentation of all of these things, as well as all of their hours, you are protected legally.
